feedback.text: Im writing to express my strong support for the Digital Fairness Act and its efforts to address anti consumer practices in the video game industry. I would also urge policymakers to include provisions that protect consumers from losing access to online games theyve paid for. Currently, publishers can shut down servers and make games unplayable, even for paying customers. This not only harms consumers, but also threatens the preservation of video games as a form of cultural and artistic expression. Ensuring that games have an end of life plan to ensure long term access would be an important step toward fairness and respect for players rights. The European Stop Killing Games initiative has already gathered over 1.4 million signatures, showing that this is an issue of serious concern to citizens across the EU.
